config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar negara atau mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Di Indonesia, saya melihat terlalu mudah orang atas nama “kepentingan umum” mengabaikan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlakuan adil terhadap orang kecil. Bagi saya, prioritas yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan: masyarakat kuat, tapi hak individu tetap dilindungi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter greatly, but they should not automatically override the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize social order, stability, and collective goals, because those values can help a country function and protect many people at once. But I also think that when “society first” becomes a blanket excuse, it can easily hide abuse of power and leave ordinary people with no real protection. A healthy society should serve people, not treat individual rights as disposable whenever the majority or the state claims a higher purpose.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh ditempatkan di atas hak individu secara otomatis. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya besar ketika “demi umum” dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil, membungkam kritik, atau membenarkan ketidakadilan atas nama mayoritas. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling keras mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga ketertiban sambil tetap melindungi martabat, kebebasan, dan perlakuan adil setiap orang. Jadi, saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan sosial selalu harus didahulukan; yang benar adalah mencari batas yang jelas agar kolektif tidak berubah menjadi alat penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should be important, but they should not automatically come before the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective responsibility, because a large society does need coordination and shared rules to function well. But I do not accept the idea that “society first” should become a blank check for officials or the majority to ignore fairness, due process, or basic dignity. In practice, when individual rights are weak, the people who suffer most are usually ordinary citizens, not the powerful, so protecting rights is not selfish; it is part of protecting society itself.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat realitas di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga biasa, sementara yang kuat tetap aman dan yang lemah yang menanggung akibatnya. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi justru akan sehat kalau dibatasi oleh hak individu, karena tanpa perlindungan itu mayoritas dan negara mudah semena-mena. Jadi bagi saya, yang benar bukan mengorbankan individu demi masyarakat, melainkan menjaga keseimbangan agar masyarakat maju tanpa menghapus martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people put weight on stability, social harmony, and collective goals, but I have also seen how easily “for the greater good” can become a justification for ignoring fairness and ordinary people’s dignity. When individual rights are weak, the people who suffer first are usually those with the least power, while the powerful can always claim they are serving society. So I see individual rights not as a selfish obstacle, but as a necessary limit that keeps society from turning into unchecked control.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
